concerns.text: The 'Verified Source' URLs provided for GSN and SORCE/TSI point to generic or unrelated HuggingFace datasets (e.g., 'silso-sunspot-number' described as construction safety, 'sorce-1k' as generic JSONL). The plan explicitly acknowledges in the 'Critical Note' that these URLs do not contain the required solar variables. Using these specific URLs as the primary data source for training a TSI reconstruction model is infeasible; the pipeline will fail to load the necessary 'sunspot_number' and 'tsi_value' columns. The plan must cite the actual canonical sources (e.g., SILSO Royal Observatory of Belgium, NASA SORCE/TIM data portals) or provide verified HuggingFace datasets that explicitly contain the required solar physics variables.

The CMIP6 v3.2 dataset URL points to a test artifact ('test-00000-of-00012.parquet') which is unlikely to contain the full historical TSI forcing data required for the 'overlapping period' correlation analysis described in User Story 2. Relying on a test shard for scientific validation violates the requirement for a complete, representative dataset. The plan must cite the full CMIP6 forcing dataset source or a verified archive containing the specific TSI forcing time series.
